description_long: This is a dilapidated, whitewashed church with a prominent central bell tower topped by a steep, pointed roof. The structure features a large arched entrance at the front, flanked by crumbling walls and missing roof sections, revealing exposed wooden beams. The roof is mostly wooden shingles, many of which are broken or missing, and the walls show significant wear, with patches of exposed brick and plaster. A circular window with a decorative frame is set above the entrance, and the side walls have arched openings, some partially collapsed. The bell tower rises above the main structure, with open archways and a visible belfry, but no bell is present. The overall appearance suggests abandonment and significant weathering, with debris and partial wall remnants extending from the main building.
description: A weathered, partially ruined white church with a tall bell tower, broken wooden shingle roof, and crumbling walls. The structure features arched entrances, a circular window, and extensive signs of decay.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched entrance, circular window, and the main bell tower with extensive roof and wall damage.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the building, the open archways of the bell tower, and collapsed wall sections.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back wall, more missing roof sections, and the circular window from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the layered roof structure, additional wall damage, and the rear entrance.
